text.title: Reference Program Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Reference Program Playbook` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Reference asks MUST happen at peak satisfaction moment (mid-engagement win, public outcome) NOT at engagement end. End-of-engagement asks have <= 30% yes rate; peak-moment asks >= 70%.

rationale.source: Win Without Pitching reference workflow; agency casework

rule.rationale: End of engagement is the worst moment — client is in transition, mind already on next thing. Peak-moment asks capture maximum emotional credit.

rule.statement: Reference roster MUST tier requests: Tier-1 (named in case study + on-call), Tier-2 (named, no calls), Tier-3 (anonymous case study). Each client picked for the tier they will sustain.
rule.rationale: Single-tier (all-or-nothing) reference asks lose moderately-happy clients. Tiered asks let lukewarm clients say yes to something instead of no to everything.

rule.statement: Case studies MUST lead with the outcome metric (revenue lift, time saved, churn reduced), not the process. Process-led studies read like SOWs, outcome-led read like proof.
rule.rationale: Buyers scanning case studies care about 'will this work for me'. Process leads with 'how' (irrelevant to prospect); outcome leads with 'what changed' (the relevant signal).

rule.statement: Reference calls MUST come with a 3-question script that the reference reviews + approves before any prospect call. Unscripted calls leak negative or off-topic content.

rationale.source: Reference-selling field manual; agency interview corpus

rule.rationale: Unscripted reference calls go off-rails — reference mentions a billing dispute, an out-of-scope ask, an old version of the product. Scripted = predictable + reuseable.

rule.statement: Warm intro asks MUST use double opt-in (separate intro to reference first, get green light, then intro to prospect). Single-step intros forbidden — burns reference goodwill.
rule.rationale: Single-step intros impose on reference's time + risk reputation. Double opt-in respects both sides + signals professionalism. References give more intros to founders who run it this way.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
